original_rubric_note: Produced before monorepo migration, when the rubric lived at E:/AI/cognitive-load-audit/cognitive-load-audit.md.

context.task: audit narrow/responsive power preservation, validate Section 5 sub-taxonomy
context.dense_state: active repo with 10 open PRs, 61 workflow runs, 97 commits, full file tree (8 dirs + README.md and translations + CHANGELOG)
context.low_bandwidth_state: auditor not in a self-reported constrained state — see OQ-04 carried from PT0
ai_features_present: no AI features exercised in this audit
context.method: Path 1 live navigation at full width + DOM/CSS class inference for narrow behavior (rig could not reduce viewport below ~2266 CSS px)
context.viewport_render_limitation: Windows 150% display scaling + Chrome min-window-width clamp prevented inner viewport reflow below 2266 CSS px; narrow findings carry Inferred evidence state

text_load.notes: Per-file commit message truncation at full width already drops info; narrow worsens. One Low. No process gap.

open_questions: OQ-01 (carried from PT0 — Lighthouse/axe still not run)
measurable_defaults.notes: No first-hand CSS measurement. Visual inspection acceptable.

hidden_load.notes: Global nav permanently in hamburger even at wide viewports — a constant Hidden pattern, not a narrow-mode delta. One Low. CL-09 and CL-12 also touch Section 2 but are filed under Section 5 as their primary axis.

state_shift.notes: Not applicable. Narrow mode is layout-driven by viewport, not a user-toggled state. No State Shift surface tested.

ai_compression_risk.notes: Not applicable. No AI surfaces exercised in this audit (Copilot present but not invoked).

power_preservation.notes: Four findings: one High (CL-09 repo nav overflow), three Medium (CL-10 PR sidebar reflow, CL-11 About sidebar reflow, CL-12 file tree collapse). Below 3-Highs Fail threshold. No Critical / no Removed-power findings observed on GitHub web. Section 5 sub-taxonomy (Compressed/Delayed/Hidden/Removed) validated as useful — see v0.2 recommendation.

configuration_cost.notes: Narrow mode is automatic from viewport, not user-configured. Not exercised.

evidence.notes: Most Section 5 findings carry Inferred evidence due to viewport-render limitation on the test rig. Full-width direct observation + DOM/CSS class inspection. Findings hold in shape; specific pixel/spacing measurements would require a re-run on a non-DPI-scaled rig or via headless browser with explicit viewport metrics.

findings.title: Repo nav uses overflow-into-'...' menu at narrow widths
findings.evidence_summary: Full-width DOM confirmed 8 tabs in `prc-components-UnderlineWrapper`; narrow-width overflow behavior is Primer's documented ResizeObserver pattern. Could not directly render at narrow widths (see OQ-05).

findings.title: PR right sidebar reflows above/below main thread at narrow widths
findings.evidence_summary: Full-width DOM confirmed 8 widget sections in right rail at PR #10; narrow stacks via Primer/Bootstrap grid behavior. No skip-link observed for narrow-mode.

findings.title: Repo About sidebar stacks below file tree + README at narrow widths
findings.evidence_summary: Full-width About sidebar with 15 topics + license/CoC/contributing/security quick-links. Bootstrap grid stacking behavior inferred for narrow.

findings.title: File tree collapses to toggle in file view at narrow widths
findings.evidence_summary: Full-width file tree confirmed at README.md view; `ExpandFileTreeButton-module__expand` class observed in DOM, GitHub's documented collapse-on-navigate pattern.

findings.title: Per-file commit message in repo home file tree is truncated even at full width
findings.evidence_summary: Direct: '.github' row shows 'fix: Stage B Wave 3 — proactive resilience + supply-chain hyg…' truncated mid-word at 2266 CSS px.

findings.title: Global account nav permanently in hamburger even at desktop widths
findings.evidence_summary: Direct: hamburger click revealed 11 items at 2266 CSS px desktop width; identical behavior expected at narrow widths.

open_questions.summary: Could not reduce inner viewport below 2266 CSS px on test rig due to Windows 150% display scaling + Chrome min-window-width clamp. Narrow-mode findings carry Inferred evidence state.
open_questions.resolution_path: Re-run on machine without DPI scaling, OR with Chrome DevTools device emulation enabled via the DevTools toolbar (Ctrl+Shift+M), OR via headless browser (Playwright/Puppeteer) with setViewport({width: 375, height: 667}).

positive_observations.summary: No Removed-power instances observed on GitHub web. Every power feature traces to Compressed, Delayed, or Hidden. GitHub's narrow-mode strategy is overflow-and-stack, not feature subset — strong Section 5 behavior.
Hamburger menu hides global nav (not repo nav). The repo-specific surface is structurally protected from being subsumed into global overflow — a load-bearing power-preservation decision.

exit_criteria_check.every_removed_power_claim_names_feature_: true (zero Removed claims; pattern not present on target)
exit_criteria_check.section_5_taxonomy_validated_or_rejected: validated as useful — adopt in rubric v0.2

rubric_v0_2_recommendations: Adopt the Compressed / Delayed / Hidden / Removed sub-taxonomy in Section 5 of the rubric. Add as a 'Severity classification for this section' block similar to Section 4's precondition.
Default severity mapping: Compressed = Pass/Low, Delayed = Medium, Hidden = High, Removed = Critical. Auditors can override with justification.
Add an explicit field to each finding: `section_5_taxonomy` (one of compressed/delayed/hidden/removed/n_a) when the finding falls under Section 5.

Note in Section 7 (Evidence) that responsive/narrow-mode audits may require a non-DPI-scaled rig or headless browser to reach the actual narrow viewport. Inferred evidence is acceptable for class-based analysis but should be flagged.
